author: Augustine of Hippo
book: Expositions on the Book of Psalms.

classification.cautionReason: No major mainstream heresy warning is attached by the preview classifier.

lineage: Expositions on the Book of Psalms.

ccel.title: NPNF1-08. St. Augustine: Exposition on the Book of Psalms

source.title: NPNF1-08. St. Augustine: Exposition on the Book of Psalms

title: Psalm LXXX

source.title: NPNF1-08. St. Augustine: Exposition on the Book of Psalms

verses.book: Expositions on the Book of Psalms.
verses.text: 1. …If perchance things obscure demand the office of an interpreter, those things which are evident ought to require of me the office of a reader.

verses.book: Expositions on the Book of Psalms.
verses.text: The song here is of the Advent of the Lord and of our Saviour Jesus Christ, and of His vineyard.

verses.book: Expositions on the Book of Psalms.
verses.text: But the singer of the song is that Asaph, as far as doth appear, enlightened and converted, by whose name ye know the synagogue to be signified.

verses.book: Expositions on the Book of Psalms.
verses.text: Lastly, the title of the Psalm is: “For the end in behalf of them that shall be changed;” that is, for the better.

verses.book: Expositions on the Book of Psalms.
verses.text: For Christ, the end of the Law, hath come on purpose that He should change men for the better.

verses.book: Expositions on the Book of Psalms.
verses.text: And he addeth, “a testimony to Asaph himself.”

verses.book: Expositions on the Book of Psalms.
verses.text: A good testimony of truth.

verses.book: Expositions on the Book of Psalms.
verses.text: Lastly, this testimony doth confess both Christ and the vineyard; that is, Head and Body, King and people, Shepherd and flock, and the entire mystery of all Scriptures, Christ and the Church.

verses.book: Expositions on the Book of Psalms.
verses.text: But the title of the Psalm doth conclude with, “for the Assyrians.”

verses.book: Expositions on the Book of Psalms.
verses.text: The Assyrians are interpreted, “men guiding.”

verses.book: Expositions on the Book of Psalms.
verses.text: Therefore it is no longer a generation which hath not guided the heart thereof, but now a generation guiding.

verses.book: Expositions on the Book of Psalms.
verses.text: Therefore hear we what he saith in this testimony.

verses.book: Expositions on the Book of Psalms.
verses.text: What is, “Thou that feedest Israel, hearken, Thou that conducteth Joseph like sheep”? (ver.

verses.book: Expositions on the Book of Psalms.
verses.text: He is being invoked to come, He is being expected until He come, He is being yearned for until He come.

verses.book: Expositions on the Book of Psalms.
verses.text: Therefore may He find “men guiding:” “Thou that conductest,” he saith, “Joseph like sheep:” Joseph himself like sheep.

verses.book: Expositions on the Book of Psalms.
verses.text: Joseph himself are the sheep, and Joseph himself is a sheep.

verses.book: Expositions on the Book of Psalms.
verses.text: Observe Joseph; for although even the interpretation of his name doth aid us much, for it signifieth increase; and He came indeed in order that the grain given to death might arise manifold; that is, that the people of God might be increased.…“Thou that sittest upon the Cherubin.”

verses.book: Expositions on the Book of Psalms.
verses.text: Cherubin is the seat of the glory of God, and is interpreted the fulness of knowledge.

verses.book: Expositions on the Book of Psalms.
verses.text: There God sitteth in the fulness of knowledge.

verses.book: Expositions on the Book of Psalms.
verses.text: Though we understand the Cherubin to be the exalted powers and virtues of the heavens: yet, if thou wilt, thou wilt be Cherubin.

verses.book: Expositions on the Book of Psalms.
verses.text: For if Cherubin is the seat of God, hear what saith the Scripture: “The soul of a just man is the seat of wisdom.”

verses.book: Expositions on the Book of Psalms.
verses.text: How, thou sayest, shall I be the fulness of knowledge?

verses.book: Expositions on the Book of Psalms.
verses.text: Who shall fulfil this?

verses.book: Expositions on the Book of Psalms.
verses.text: Thou hast the means of fulfilling it: “The fulness of the Law is love.”

verses.book: Expositions on the Book of Psalms.
verses.text: Do not run after many things, and strain thyself.

verses.book: Expositions on the Book of Psalms.
verses.text: The amplitude of the branches doth terrify thee: hold by the root, and of the greatness of the tree think not.

verses.book: Expositions on the Book of Psalms.
verses.text: Be there in thee love, and the fulness of knowledge must needs follow.

verses.book: Expositions on the Book of Psalms.
verses.text: For what doth he not know that knoweth love?

verses.book: Expositions on the Book of Psalms.
verses.text: Inasmuch as it hath been said, “God is love.”

verses.book: Expositions on the Book of Psalms.
verses.text: For we went astray because Thou didst not appear.

verses.book: Expositions on the Book of Psalms.
verses.text: “Before Ephraim and Benjamin and Manasse” (ver.

verses.book: Expositions on the Book of Psalms.
verses.text: Appear, I say, before the nation of the Jews, before the people of Israel.

verses.book: Expositions on the Book of Psalms.
verses.text: For there is Ephraim, there Manasses, there Benjamin.

verses.book: Expositions on the Book of Psalms.
verses.text: But to the interpretation let us look: Ephraim is fruit-bearing, Benjamin son of right hand, Manasses one forgetful.

verses.book: Expositions on the Book of Psalms.
verses.text: Appear Thou then before one made fruitful, before a son of the right hand: appear Thou before one forgetful, in order that he may be no longer forgetful, but Thou mayest come into his mind that hast delivered him.…For weak Thou wast when it was being said, “If Son of God He is, let Him come down from the Cross.”

verses.book: Expositions on the Book of Psalms.
verses.text: Thou wast seeming to have no power: the persecutor had power over Thee: and Thou didst show this aforetime, for Jacob too himself prevailed in wrestling, a man with an angel.

verses.book: Expositions on the Book of Psalms.
verses.text: Would he at any time, except the angel had been willing?

verses.book: Expositions on the Book of Psalms.
verses.text: And man prevailed, and the angel was conquered: and victorious man holdeth the angel, and saith, “I will not let thee go, except thou shalt have blessed me.”

verses.book: Expositions on the Book of Psalms.
verses.text: A great sacrament!

verses.book: Expositions on the Book of Psalms.
verses.text: He both standeth conquered, and blesseth the conqueror.

verses.book: Expositions on the Book of Psalms.
verses.text: Conquered, because he willed it; in flesh weak, in majesty strong.…Having been crucified of weakness, rise Thou in power: “Stir up Thy power, and come Thou, to save us.”

verses.book: Expositions on the Book of Psalms.
verses.text: “O God, convert us.”

verses.book: Expositions on the Book of Psalms.
verses.text: For averse we have been from Thee, and except Thou convert us, we shall not be converted.

verses.book: Expositions on the Book of Psalms.
verses.text: “And illumine Thy face, and we shall be saved” (ver.

verses.book: Expositions on the Book of Psalms.
verses.text: Hath He anywise a darkened face?

verses.book: Expositions on the Book of Psalms.
verses.text: He hath not a darkened face, but He placed before it a cloud of flesh, and as it were a veil of weakness; and when He hung on the tree, He was not thought the Same as He was after to be acknowledged when He was sitting in Heaven.

verses.book: Expositions on the Book of Psalms.
verses.text: For thus it hath come to pass.

verses.book: Expositions on the Book of Psalms.
verses.text: Christ present on the earth, and doing miracles, Asaph knew not; but when He had died, after that He rose again, and ascended into Heaven, he knew Him.

verses.book: Expositions on the Book of Psalms.
verses.text: He was pricked to the heart, and he may have spoken also of Him this testimony which now we acknowledge in this Psalm.

verses.book: Expositions on the Book of Psalms.
verses.text: Thou didst cover Thy face, and we were sick: illumine Thou the same, and we shall be whole.

verses.book: Expositions on the Book of Psalms.
verses.text: “O Lord God of virtues, how long wilt Thou be angry with the prayer of Thy servant?” (ver.

verses.book: Expositions on the Book of Psalms.
verses.text: Now Thy servant.

verses.book: Expositions on the Book of Psalms.
verses.text: Thou wast angry at the prayer of Thy enemy, wilt Thou still be angry with the prayer of Thy servant?

verses.book: Expositions on the Book of Psalms.
verses.text: Thou hast converted us, we know Thee, and wilt Thou still be angry with the prayer of Thy servant?

verses.book: Expositions on the Book of Psalms.
verses.text: Thou wilt evidently be angry, in fact, as a father correcting, not as a judge condemning.

verses.book: Expositions on the Book of Psalms.
verses.text: In such manner evidently Thou wilt be angry, because it hath been written, “My son, drawing near unto the service of God, stand thou in righteousness and in fear, and prepare thy soul for temptation.”

verses.book: Expositions on the Book of Psalms.
verses.text: Think not that now the wrath of God hath passed away, because thou hast been converted.

verses.book: Expositions on the Book of Psalms.
verses.text: The wrath of God hath passed away from thee, but only so that it condemn not for everlasting.

verses.book: Expositions on the Book of Psalms.
verses.text: But He scourgeth, He spareth not: because He scourgeth every son whom He receiveth.

verses.book: Expositions on the Book of Psalms.
verses.text: If thou refusest to be scourged, why dost thou desire to be received?

verses.book: Expositions on the Book of Psalms.
verses.text: He scourgeth every son whom He receiveth.

verses.book: Expositions on the Book of Psalms.
verses.text: He who did not spare even His only Son, scourgeth every one.

verses.book: Expositions on the Book of Psalms.
verses.text: But nevertheless, “How long wilt Thou be angry with the prayer of Thy servant?”

verses.book: Expositions on the Book of Psalms.
verses.text: No longer thine enemy: but, “Thou wilt be angry with the prayer of Thy servant,” how long?

verses.book: Expositions on the Book of Psalms.
verses.text: There followeth: “Thou wilt feed us with the bread of tears, and wilt give us to drink with tears in measure” (ver.

verses.book: Expositions on the Book of Psalms.
verses.text: What is, “in measure”?

verses.book: Expositions on the Book of Psalms.
verses.text: Hear the Apostle: “Faithful is God, who doth not suffer you to be tempted above that ye are able to bear.”

verses.book: Expositions on the Book of Psalms.
verses.text: The measure is, according to your powers: the measure is, that thou be instructed, not that thou be crushed.

verses.book: Expositions on the Book of Psalms.
verses.text: “Thou hast set us for a contradiction to our neighbours” (ver.

verses.book: Expositions on the Book of Psalms.
verses.text: Evidently this did come to pass: for out of Asaph were chosen they that should go to the Gentiles and preach Christ, and should have it said to them, “Who is this proclaimer of new demons?”

verses.book: Expositions on the Book of Psalms.
verses.text: “Thou hast set us for a contradiction to our neighbours.”

verses.book: Expositions on the Book of Psalms.
verses.text: For they were preaching Him who was the subject of the contradiction.

verses.book: Expositions on the Book of Psalms.
verses.text: Whom did they preach?

verses.book: Expositions on the Book of Psalms.
verses.text: That after He was dead, Christ rose again.

verses.book: Expositions on the Book of Psalms.
verses.text: Who would hear this?

verses.book: Expositions on the Book of Psalms.
verses.text: Who would know this?

verses.book: Expositions on the Book of Psalms.
verses.text: It is a new thing.

verses.book: Expositions on the Book of Psalms.
verses.text: But signs did follow, and to an incredible thing miracles gave credibility.

verses.book: Expositions on the Book of Psalms.
verses.text: He was contradicted, but the contradictor was conquered, and from being a contradictor was made a believer.

verses.book: Expositions on the Book of Psalms.
verses.text: There, however, was a great flame: there the martyrs fed with the bread of tears, and given to drink in tears, but in measure, not more than they are able to bear; in order that after the measure of tears there should follow a crown of joys.

verses.book: Expositions on the Book of Psalms.
verses.text: “And our enemies have sneered at us.”

verses.book: Expositions on the Book of Psalms.
verses.text: And where are they that sneered?

verses.book: Expositions on the Book of Psalms.
verses.text: For a long while it was said, Who are they that worship the Dead One, that adore the Crucified?

verses.book: Expositions on the Book of Psalms.
verses.text: For a long while so it was said.

verses.book: Expositions on the Book of Psalms.
verses.text: Where is the nose of them that sneered?

verses.book: Expositions on the Book of Psalms.
verses.text: Now do not they that censure flee into caves, that they may not be seen?

verses.book: Expositions on the Book of Psalms.
verses.text: But ye see what followeth: “O Lord God of virtues, convert us, and show Thy face, and we shall be whole” (ver.

verses.book: Expositions on the Book of Psalms.
verses.text: “A vineyard out of Egypt Thou hast brought over, Thou hast cast out the nations, and hast planted her” (ver.

verses.book: Expositions on the Book of Psalms.
verses.text: It was done, we know.

verses.book: Expositions on the Book of Psalms.
verses.text: How many nations were cast out?

verses.book: Expositions on the Book of Psalms.
verses.text: Amorites, Cethites, Jebusites, Gergesites, and Evites: after whose expulsion and overthrow, there was led in the people delivered out of Egypt, into the land of promise.

verses.book: Expositions on the Book of Psalms.
verses.text: Whence the vineyard was cast out, and where she was planted, we have heard.

verses.book: Expositions on the Book of Psalms.
verses.text: Let us see what next was done, how she believed, how much she grew, what ground she covered.

verses.book: Expositions on the Book of Psalms.
verses.text: “A way Thou hast made in the sight of her, and hast planted the roots of her, and she hath filled the land” (ver.

verses.book: Expositions on the Book of Psalms.
verses.text: Would she have filled the land, unless a way had been made in the sight of her?

verses.book: Expositions on the Book of Psalms.
verses.text: What was the way which was made in the sight of her?

verses.book: Expositions on the Book of Psalms.
verses.text: “I am,” He saith, “the Way, the Truth, and the Life.”

verses.book: Expositions on the Book of Psalms.
verses.text: With reason she hath filled the land.

verses.book: Expositions on the Book of Psalms.
verses.text: That hath now been said of this vineyard, which hath been accomplished at the last.

verses.book: Expositions on the Book of Psalms.
verses.text: But in the mean time what?

verses.book: Expositions on the Book of Psalms.
verses.text: “She hath covered the mountains with her shadow, and with her branch the cedars of God” (ver.

verses.book: Expositions on the Book of Psalms.
verses.text: “Thou hast stretched out her boughs even unto the sea, and even unto the river her shoots” (ver.

verses.book: Expositions on the Book of Psalms.
verses.text: This requireth the office of an expositor, that of a reader and praiser doth not suffice: aid me with attention; for the mention of this vineyard in this Psalm is wont to overcloud with darkness the inattentive.…But nevertheless the first Jewish nation was this vine.

verses.book: Expositions on the Book of Psalms.
verses.text: But the Jewish nation reigned as far as the sea and as far as the river.

verses.book: Expositions on the Book of Psalms.
verses.text: As far as the sea; it appeareth in Scripture that the sea was in the vicinity thereof.

verses.book: Expositions on the Book of Psalms.
verses.text: And as far as the river Jordan.

verses.book: Expositions on the Book of Psalms.
verses.text: For on the other side of Jordan some part of the Jews was established, but within Jordan was the whole nation.

verses.book: Expositions on the Book of Psalms.
verses.text: Therefore, “even unto the sea and even unto the river,” is the kingdom of the Jews, the kingdom of Israel: but not “from sea even unto sea, and from the river even unto the ends of the round world;” this is the future perfection of the vineyard, concerning which in this place he hath foretold.

verses.book: Expositions on the Book of Psalms.
verses.text: When, I say, he had foretold to thee the perfection, he returneth to the beginning, out of which the perfection was made.

verses.book: Expositions on the Book of Psalms.
verses.text: Of the beginning wilt thou hear?

verses.book: Expositions on the Book of Psalms.
verses.text: “Even unto the river.”

verses.book: Expositions on the Book of Psalms.
verses.text: Of the end wilt thou hear?

verses.book: Expositions on the Book of Psalms.
verses.text: “He shall have dominion from sea even unto sea:” that is, “she hath filled the earth.”

verses.book: Expositions on the Book of Psalms.
verses.text: Let us look then to the testimony of Asaph, as to what was done to the first vineyard, and what must be expected for the second vineyard, nay to the same vineyard.…What then, the vineyard before the sight whereof a way was made, that she should fill the earth, at first was where?

verses.book: Expositions on the Book of Psalms.
verses.text: “Her shadow covered the mountains.”

verses.book: Expositions on the Book of Psalms.
verses.text: Who are the mountains?

verses.book: Expositions on the Book of Psalms.
verses.text: The Prophets.

verses.book: Expositions on the Book of Psalms.
verses.text: Why did her shadow cover them?

verses.book: Expositions on the Book of Psalms.
verses.text: Because darkly they spake the things which were foretold as to come.

verses.book: Expositions on the Book of Psalms.
verses.text: Thou hearest from the Prophets, Keep the Sabbath-day, on the eighth day circumcise a child, offer sacrifice of ram, of calf, of he-goat.

verses.book: Expositions on the Book of Psalms.
verses.text: Be not troubled, her shadow doth cover the mountains of God; there will come after the shadow a manifestation.

verses.book: Expositions on the Book of Psalms.
verses.text: “And her shrubs the cedars of God,” that is, she hath covered the cedars of God; very lofty, but of God.

verses.book: Expositions on the Book of Psalms.
verses.text: For the cedars are types of the proud, that must needs be overthrown.

verses.book: Expositions on the Book of Psalms.
verses.text: The “cedars of Lebanon,” the heights of the world, this vineyard did cover in growing, and the mountains of God, all the holy Prophets and Patriarchs.

verses.book: Expositions on the Book of Psalms.
verses.text: “Wherefore hast Thou thrown down her enclosure?” (ver.

verses.book: Expositions on the Book of Psalms.
verses.text: Now ye see the overthrow of that nation of the Jews: already out of another Psalm ye have heard, “with axe and hammer they have thrown her down.”

verses.book: Expositions on the Book of Psalms.
verses.text: When could this have been done, except her enclosure had been thrown down.

verses.book: Expositions on the Book of Psalms.
verses.text: What is her enclosure?

verses.book: Expositions on the Book of Psalms.
verses.text: Her defence.

verses.book: Expositions on the Book of Psalms.
verses.text: For she bore herself proudly against her planter.

verses.book: Expositions on the Book of Psalms.
verses.text: The servants that were sent to her and demanded a recompense, the husbandmen they scourged, beat, slew: there came also the Only Son, they said, “This is the Heir; come, let us kill Him, and our own the inheritance will be:” they killed Him, and out of the vineyard they cast Him forth.

verses.book: Expositions on the Book of Psalms.
verses.text: When cast forth, He did more perfectly possess the place whence He was cast forth.

verses.book: Expositions on the Book of Psalms.
verses.text: For thus He threatens her through Isaiah, “I will throw down her enclosure.”

verses.book: Expositions on the Book of Psalms.
verses.text: “For I looked that she should bring forth grapes, but she brought forth thorns.”

verses.book: Expositions on the Book of Psalms.
verses.text: I looked for fruit from thence, and I found sin.

verses.book: Expositions on the Book of Psalms.
verses.text: Why then dost thou ask, O Asaph, “Why hast Thou thrown down her enclosure?”

verses.book: Expositions on the Book of Psalms.
verses.text: For knowest thou not why?

verses.book: Expositions on the Book of Psalms.
verses.text: I looked that she should do judgment, and she did iniquity.

verses.book: Expositions on the Book of Psalms.
verses.text: Must not her enclosure needs be thrown down?

verses.book: Expositions on the Book of Psalms.
verses.text: And there came the Gentiles when the enclosure was thrown down, the vineyard was assailed, and the kingdom of the Jews effaced.

verses.book: Expositions on the Book of Psalms.
verses.text: This at first he is lamenting, but not without hope.

verses.book: Expositions on the Book of Psalms.
verses.text: For of directing the heart he is now speaking, that is, for the “Assyrians,” for “men directing,” the Psalm is.

verses.book: Expositions on the Book of Psalms.
verses.text: “Wherefore hast Thou thrown down her enclosure: and there pluck off her grapes all men passing along the way.”

verses.book: Expositions on the Book of Psalms.
verses.text: What is “men passing along the way?”

verses.book: Expositions on the Book of Psalms.
verses.text: Men having dominion for a time.

verses.book: Expositions on the Book of Psalms.
verses.text: “There hath laid her waste the boar from the wood” (ver.

verses.book: Expositions on the Book of Psalms.
verses.text: In the boar from the wood what do we understand?

verses.book: Expositions on the Book of Psalms.
verses.text: To the Jews a swine is an abomination, and in a swine they imagine as it were the uncleanness of the Gentiles.

verses.book: Expositions on the Book of Psalms.
verses.text: But by the Gentiles was overthrown the nation of the Jews: but that king who overthrew, was not only an unclean swine, but was also a boar.

verses.book: Expositions on the Book of Psalms.
verses.text: For what is a boar but a savage swine, a furious swine?

verses.book: Expositions on the Book of Psalms.
verses.text: “A boar from the wood hath laid her waste.”

verses.book: Expositions on the Book of Psalms.
verses.text: “From the wood,” from the Gentiles.

verses.book: Expositions on the Book of Psalms.
verses.text: For she was a vineyard, but the Gentiles were woods.

verses.book: Expositions on the Book of Psalms.
verses.text: But when the Gentiles believed, there was said what?

verses.book: Expositions on the Book of Psalms.
verses.text: “Then there shall exult all the trees of the woods.”

verses.book: Expositions on the Book of Psalms.
verses.text: “The boar from the wood hath laid her waste; and a singular wild beast hath devoured her.”

verses.book: Expositions on the Book of Psalms.
verses.text: “A singular wild beast” is what?

verses.book: Expositions on the Book of Psalms.
verses.text: The very boar that laid her waste is the singular wild beast.

verses.book: Expositions on the Book of Psalms.
verses.text: Singular, because proud.

verses.book: Expositions on the Book of Psalms.
verses.text: For thus saith every proud one, It is I, it is I, and no other.

verses.book: Expositions on the Book of Psalms.
verses.text: But with what profit is this?

verses.book: Expositions on the Book of Psalms.
verses.text: “O God of virtues turn Thou nevertheless” (ver.

verses.book: Expositions on the Book of Psalms.
verses.text: Although these things have been done, “Turn Thou nevertheless.”

verses.book: Expositions on the Book of Psalms.
verses.text: “Look from heaven and see, and visit this vineyard.”

verses.book: Expositions on the Book of Psalms.
verses.text: “And perfect Thou her whom Thy right hand hath planted” (ver.

verses.book: Expositions on the Book of Psalms.
verses.text: No other plant Thou, but this make Thou perfect.

verses.book: Expositions on the Book of Psalms.
verses.text: For she is the very seed of Abraham, she is the very seed in whom all nations shall be blessed: there is the root where is borne the graffed wild olive.

verses.book: Expositions on the Book of Psalms.
verses.text: “Perfect Thou this vineyard which Thy right hand hath planted.”

verses.book: Expositions on the Book of Psalms.
verses.text: But wherein doth He perfect?

verses.book: Expositions on the Book of Psalms.
verses.text: “And upon the Son of man, whom Thou hast strengthened to Thyself.”

verses.book: Expositions on the Book of Psalms.
verses.text: What can be more evident?

verses.book: Expositions on the Book of Psalms.
verses.text: Why do ye still expect, that we should still explain to you in discourse, and should we not rather cry out with you in admiration, “Perfect Thou this vineyard which Thy right hand hath planted, and upon the Son of man” perfect her?

verses.book: Expositions on the Book of Psalms.
verses.text: What Son of man?

verses.book: Expositions on the Book of Psalms.
verses.text: Him “whom Thou hast strengthened to Thyself.”

verses.book: Expositions on the Book of Psalms.
verses.text: A mighty stronghold: build as much as thou art able.

verses.book: Expositions on the Book of Psalms.
verses.text: “For other foundation no one is able to lay, except that which is laid, which is Christ Jesus.”

verses.book: Expositions on the Book of Psalms.
verses.text: “Things burned with fire, and dug up, by the rebuke of Thy countenance shall perish” (ver.

verses.book: Expositions on the Book of Psalms.
verses.text: What are the things burned with fire and dug up which shall perish from the rebuke of His countenance?

verses.book: Expositions on the Book of Psalms.
verses.text: Let us see and perceive what are the things burned with fire and dug up.

verses.book: Expositions on the Book of Psalms.
verses.text: Christ hath rebuked what?

verses.book: Expositions on the Book of Psalms.
verses.text: Sins: by the rebuke of His countenance sins have perished.

verses.book: Expositions on the Book of Psalms.
verses.text: Why then are sins burned with fire and dug up?

verses.book: Expositions on the Book of Psalms.
verses.text: Of all sins, two things are the cause in man, desire and fear.

verses.book: Expositions on the Book of Psalms.
verses.text: Think, examine, question your hearts, sift your consciences, see whether there can be sins, except they be either of desire, or of fear.

verses.book: Expositions on the Book of Psalms.
verses.text: There is set before thee a reward to induce thee to sin, that is, a thing which delighteth thee; thou doest it, because thou desirest it.

verses.book: Expositions on the Book of Psalms.
verses.text: But perchance thou wilt not be allured by bribes; thou art terrified with menaces, thou doest it because thou fearest.

verses.book: Expositions on the Book of Psalms.
verses.text: A man would bribe thee, for example, to bear false witness.

verses.book: Expositions on the Book of Psalms.
verses.text: Countless cases there are, but I am setting before you the plainer cases, whereby ye may imagine the rest.

verses.book: Expositions on the Book of Psalms.
verses.text: Hast thou hearkened unto God, and hast thou said in thy heart, “What doth it profit a man, if he gain the whole world, but of his own soul suffer loss?”

verses.book: Expositions on the Book of Psalms.
verses.text: I am not allured by a bribe to lose my soul to gain money.

verses.book: Expositions on the Book of Psalms.
verses.text: He turneth himself to stir up fear within thee, he who was not able to corrupt thee with a bribe, beginneth to threaten loss, banishment, massacres, perchance, and death.

verses.book: Expositions on the Book of Psalms.
verses.text: Therein now, if desire prevailed not, perchance fear will prevail to make thee sin.…What had evil fear done?

verses.book: Expositions on the Book of Psalms.
verses.text: It had dug up, as it were.

verses.book: Expositions on the Book of Psalms.
verses.text: For love doth inflame, fear doth humble: therefore, sins of evil love, with fire were lighted: sins of evil fear were dug up.

verses.book: Expositions on the Book of Psalms.
verses.text: On the one hand, evil fear doth humble, and good love doth light; but in different ways respectively.

verses.book: Expositions on the Book of Psalms.
verses.text: For even the husbandman interceding for the tree, that it should not be cut down, saith, “I will dig about it, and will apply a basket of dung.”

verses.book: Expositions on the Book of Psalms.
verses.text: The dug trench doth signify the godly humility of one fearing, and the basket of dung the profitable squalid state of one repenting.

verses.book: Expositions on the Book of Psalms.
verses.text: But concerning the fire of good love the Lord saith, “Fire I have come to send into the world.”

verses.book: Expositions on the Book of Psalms.
verses.text: With which fire may the fervent in spirit burn, and they too that are inflamed with the love of God and their neighbour.

verses.book: Expositions on the Book of Psalms.
verses.text: And thus, as all good works are wrought by good fear and good love, so by evil fear and evil love all sins are committed.

verses.book: Expositions on the Book of Psalms.
verses.text: Therefore, “Things set alight with fire and dug up,” to wit, all sins, “by the rebuke of Thy countenance shall perish.”

verses.book: Expositions on the Book of Psalms.
verses.text: “Let Thy hand be upon the Man of Thy right hand, and upon the Son of Man whom Thou hast strengthened Thyself” (ver.

verses.book: Expositions on the Book of Psalms.
verses.text: “And we depart not from Thee.…Thou wilt quicken us, and Thy Name we will invoke” (ver.

verses.book: Expositions on the Book of Psalms.
verses.text: Thou shalt be sweet to us, “Thou wilt quicken us.”

verses.book: Expositions on the Book of Psalms.
verses.text: For aforetime we did love earth, not Thee: but Thou hast mortified our members which are upon the earth.

verses.book: Expositions on the Book of Psalms.
verses.text: For the Old Testament, having earthly promises, seemeth to exhort that God should not be loved for nought, but that He should be loved because He giveth something on earth.

verses.book: Expositions on the Book of Psalms.
verses.text: What dost thou love, so as not to love God?

verses.book: Expositions on the Book of Psalms.
verses.text: Love, if thou canst, anything which He hath not made.

verses.book: Expositions on the Book of Psalms.
verses.text: Look round upon the whole creation, see whether in any place thou art held with the birdlime of desire, and hindered from loving the Creator, except it be by that very thing which He hath Himself created, whom thou despisest.

verses.book: Expositions on the Book of Psalms.
verses.text: But why dost thou love those things, except because they are beautiful?

verses.book: Expositions on the Book of Psalms.
verses.text: Can they be as beautiful as He by whom they were made?

verses.book: Expositions on the Book of Psalms.
verses.text: Thou admirest these things, because thou seest not Him: but through those things which thou admirest, love Him whom thou seest not.

verses.book: Expositions on the Book of Psalms.
verses.text: Examine the creation; if of itself it is, stay therein: but if it is of Him, for no other reason is it prejudicial to a lover, than because it is preferred to the Creator.

verses.book: Expositions on the Book of Psalms.
verses.text: Why have I said this?

verses.book: Expositions on the Book of Psalms.
verses.text: With reference to this verse, brethren.

verses.book: Expositions on the Book of Psalms.
verses.text: Dead, I say, were they that did worship God that it might be well with them after the flesh: “For to be wise after the flesh is death:” and dead are they that do not worship God gratis, that is, because of Himself He is good, not because He giveth such and such good things, which He giveth even to men not good.

verses.book: Expositions on the Book of Psalms.
verses.text: Money wilt thou have of God?

verses.book: Expositions on the Book of Psalms.
verses.text: Even a robber hath it.

verses.book: Expositions on the Book of Psalms.
verses.text: Wife, abundance of children, soundness of body, the world’s dignity, observe how many evil men have.

verses.book: Expositions on the Book of Psalms.
verses.text: Is this all for the sake of which thou dost worship Him?

verses.book: Expositions on the Book of Psalms.
verses.text: Thy feet will totter, thou wilt suppose thyself to worship without cause, when thou seest those things to be with them who do not worship Him.

verses.book: Expositions on the Book of Psalms.
verses.text: All these things, I say, He giveth even to evil men, Himself alone He reserveth for good men.

verses.book: Expositions on the Book of Psalms.
verses.text: “Thou wilt quicken us;” for dead we were, when to earthly things we did cleave; dead we were, when of the earthly man we did bear the image.

verses.book: Expositions on the Book of Psalms.
verses.text: “Thou wilt quicken us;” Thou wilt renew us, the life of the inward man Thou wilt give us.

verses.book: Expositions on the Book of Psalms.
verses.text: “And Thy Name we will invoke;” that is, Thee we will love.

verses.book: Expositions on the Book of Psalms.
verses.text: Thou to us wilt be the sweet forgiver of our sins, Thou wilt be the entire reward of the justified.

verses.book: Expositions on the Book of Psalms.
verses.text: “O Lord God of virtues, convert us, and show Thy face, and we shall be whole” (ver.
